metadata.name: Traefik
metadata.description: Swarm-only Traefik reverse proxy and load balancer with Docker Swarm service discovery, optional Let's Encrypt DNS challenge TLS, dashboard, and metrics support. For Traefik-managed ACME certificates, run a single replica; Traefik OSS ACME storage is not safe for active-active writes from multiple instances. References: Traefik Swarm provider https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/providers/swarm/ and Traefik ACME storage notes https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/https/acme/.

items.name: service_name
items.title: Service Name
items.description: Name of the Swarm service and generated resources.

items.name: container_timezone
items.title: Container Timezone
items.description: Timezone used inside the container.

items.name: container_loglevel
items.title: Log Level
items.description: Traefik log level.

items.name: ports_http
items.title: HTTP Port
items.description: Published port for the web entrypoint.

items.name: ports_https
items.title: HTTPS Port
items.description: Published port for the websecure entrypoint.

items.name: ports_dashboard
items.title: Dashboard Port
items.description: Published port for the insecure dashboard/API.

items.name: ports_metrics
items.title: Metrics Port
items.description: Published port for Prometheus metrics.

items.name: ports_publish_mode
items.title: Publish Mode
items.description: Swarm port publishing mode. Host mode is recommended for edge proxies.

items.name: traefik_network
items.title: Network
items.description: Overlay network Traefik uses to reach routed Swarm services.

items.name: traefik_network_external
items.title: External Network
items.description: Use an existing external overlay network.

items.name: accesslog_enabled
items.title: Access Log
items.description: Enable Traefik access logs.

items.name: dashboard_enabled
items.title: Dashboard
items.description: Enable the insecure Traefik dashboard/API. Do not expose this in production.

items.name: prometheus_enabled
items.title: Prometheus
items.description: Enable Prometheus metrics.

items.name: traefik_tls_enabled
items.title: TLS
items.description: Enable HTTPS/TLS with ACME DNS challenge.

items.name: traefik_tls_certresolver
items.title: DNS Provider
items.description: ACME DNS challenge provider.

items.name: traefik_tls_acme_email
items.title: ACME Email
items.description: Email address used for ACME registration.

items.name: traefik_tls_acme_token
items.title: API Token / ID
items.description: DNS provider API token or access/client ID. Stored as a Swarm secret.

items.name: traefik_tls_acme_secret_key
items.title: Secret Key
items.description: DNS provider secret key. Stored as a Swarm secret.

items.name: traefik_tls_acme_consumer_key
items.title: Consumer Key
items.description: OVH consumer key. Stored as a Swarm secret.

items.name: traefik_tls_acme_username
items.title: Username
items.description: Namecheap API username.

items.name: traefik_tls_acme_region
items.title: AWS Region
items.description: AWS region for Route53.

items.name: traefik_tls_acme_endpoint
items.title: OVH Endpoint
items.description: OVH API endpoint.

items.name: traefik_tls_acme_tenant_id
items.title: Azure Tenant ID
items.description: Azure tenant ID.

items.name: traefik_tls_acme_subscription_id
items.title: Azure Subscription ID
items.description: Azure subscription ID.

items.name: traefik_tls_acme_resource_group
items.title: Azure Resource Group
items.description: Azure DNS resource group.

items.name: traefik_tls_redirect
items.title: HTTP Redirect
items.description: Redirect all HTTP traffic to HTTPS.

items.name: traefik_tls_min_version
items.title: Minimum TLS Version
items.description: Minimum TLS version.

items.name: traefik_tls_secure_ciphers
items.title: Strict Ciphers
items.description: Enable a strict modern cipher suite list.

items.name: traefik_tls_skipverify
items.title: Skip Backend Verify
items.description: Skip TLS verification for backend servers.

items.name: swarm_placement_mode
items.title: Placement Mode
items.description: Swarm deployment mode. Traefik is constrained to manager nodes because the Swarm provider reads service state through the local Docker socket. Use replicated with one replica when Traefik manages ACME certificates. Global mode runs one task per eligible manager node, but Traefik OSS cannot safely coordinate active-active ACME writes to a shared acme.json. See https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/providers/swarm/ and https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/https/acme/.

items.name: swarm_replicas
items.title: Replicas
items.description: Number of replicas when placement mode is replicated. Keep this at 1 when Traefik manages ACME certificates; use multiple replicas only with external/static certificate management.

items.name: swarm_placement_host
items.title: Placement Host
items.description: Optional manager-node hostname constraint. The selected node must be a Swarm manager.

items.name: volume_mode
items.title: Volume Mode
items.description: Storage mode for ACME certificate data. Shared storage allows file access from multiple nodes, but it does not make Traefik-managed ACME safe with multiple active Traefik writers. Use one Traefik replica for ACME, or use external/static certificate management for multi-replica Traefik. See https://doc.traefik.io/traefik/https/acme/.

items.name: volume_mount_path
items.title: Bind Mount Path
items.description: Host path used for certificate storage.

items.name: volume_nfs_server
items.title: NFS Server
items.description: Hostname or IP address of the NFS server.

items.name: volume_nfs_path
items.title: NFS Path
items.description: Export path on the NFS server.

items.name: volume_nfs_options
items.title: NFS Options
items.description: Mount options for the NFS volume.
